text: The surgical team uses the DaVinci XI robotic system in a forearm configuration to perform a robot-assisted partial nephrectomy. The patient is positioned supine with the right flank elevated at twenty degrees and a thirty-degree Trendelenburg tilt. Four eight-millimeter robotic ports and two twelve-millimeter assistant ports are placed. The ascending colon is mobilized, exposing the isthmus of the osteokidney and the renal tumor.

The surgeon prepares the three right renal arteries, clamps the lower right artery with a bulldog clamp, and injects indocyanine green intravenously. The Firefly ICG test confirms the devascularization of the renal mass, which emits green fluorescence upon blood flow restoration. The edges of the renal tumor are assessed with an ultrasound probe, and the incision line is scored on the renal parenchyma.

The surgeon performs resection of the renal mass using cold scissors under selective ischemia. The excised mass is placed in an endo bag. Medullary sutures are applied in a running fashion with a 3-0 Monocryl stitch, secured with Hem-o-lok clips. After unclamping, a cortical suture is completed using a V-Loc suture with a sliding clip technique.
Surgiflo is applied to the renal bridge, followed by the placement of a tubular drain. The surgical field is clear, with no visible bleeding or complications.
